Package org.apache.juneau.rest.server.view.freemarker

Class FreemarkerView

java.lang.Object
org.apache.juneau.rest.server.view.freemarker.FreemarkerView
All Implemented Interfaces:
View
public final class FreemarkerView extends Object implements View
Immutable value class returned from @RestOp-annotated methods to ask the framework to render an Apache FreeMarker template.

Companion to FreemarkerMixin and FreemarkerViewRenderer: the mixin sets up the /freemarker/* mount and registers the renderer; the renderer detects FreemarkerView returns in the response-processor chain and asks the configured freemarker.template.Configuration to getTemplate(templateName) and then process(dataModel, writer) directly onto the response writer.

Usage:

@RestGet("/hello/{name}") public View hello(@Path String name) { return FreemarkerView.of("hello.ftlh") .attr("name", name) .attr("ts", Instant.now()); }

The template name is configuration-relative — the configured freemarker.template.Configuration template loader prepends its own resource root. With the bridge's default configuration + basePath("/templates/"), FreemarkerView.of("hello.ftlh") resolves to /templates/hello.ftlh on the classpath.

.ftl vs .ftlh auto-escape:

FreeMarker auto-selects HTML escaping by file extension: .ftlh templates emit HTML-escaped output (variable references are escaped automatically), while .ftl templates emit raw output. For HTML responses, prefer the .ftlh extension to avoid an XSS regression sneaking in via a future attribute-binding change.

Template suffix:

The templateSuffix(...) builder knob lets the bridge append a suffix (e.g. ".ftlh") to template names that don't already end with it, so callers can write FreemarkerView.of("hello") and have the bridge resolve it as "hello.ftlh" on the classpath (idempotent — explicit suffix is honored).

Immutability:

Each attr(...) / attrs(...) / header(...) call returns a new FreemarkerView carrying the additional binding; the original instance is unchanged. This keeps FreemarkerView a value type safe to share across requests (e.g. as a static singleton built once and returned from many handlers).

See Also:
  • FreemarkerMixin
  • FreemarkerViewRenderer
  • FreeMarker View Support
Since:
10.0.0

  • Method Details

    • of

      public static FreemarkerView of(String templateName)
      Creates a new FreemarkerView carrying the given template name and no attributes.

      The template name is configuration-relative; the configured freemarker.template.Configuration template loader supplies the prefix (resource root). With the bridge's default configuration + basePath("/templates/"), the name "hello.ftlh" resolves to /templates/hello.ftlh on the classpath.

      Parameters:
      templateName - The template name (configuration-relative, suffix optional when the mixin's templateSuffix is configured). Must not be null or blank.
      Returns:
      A new FreemarkerView instance.
      Throws:
      IllegalArgumentException - If templateName is null or blank.

    • attr

      public FreemarkerView attr(String key, Object value)
      Returns a copy of this view with the given attribute added (or replaced if a binding for key already exists).

      The full attribute map is passed to FreeMarker as the data model, so each entry is accessible inside the template as ${key}. null values are rejected at build-time because FreeMarker's default null-handling renders missing/null bindings as an error or empty string depending on configuration, which typically masks what is a caller bug; rejecting up-front surfaces the mistake at the call site (matches the Thymeleaf / Mustache bridge precedents).

      Parameters:
      key - The attribute key (exposed inside the template as ${key}). Must not be null or blank.
      value - The attribute value. Must not be null.
      Returns:
      A new FreemarkerView carrying the additional attribute.
      Throws:
      IllegalArgumentException - If key is null or blank, or value is null.

    • attrs

      public FreemarkerView attrs(Map<String,?> values)
      Returns a copy of this view with all entries from the given map added (or replaced if a binding for any key already exists).
      Parameters:
      values - The attributes to add. null is treated as an empty map (the result is the same instance with no entries added).
      Returns:
      A new FreemarkerView carrying the additional attributes.
      Throws:
      IllegalArgumentException - If any entry has a null/blank key or a null value.

    • header

      public FreemarkerView header(String name, String value)
      Returns a copy of this view with the given response header set on the rendered response.

      Typical usage: .header("Content-Type", "text/html; charset=UTF-8") or .header("Cache-Control", "no-store"). The renderer applies these headers via HttpServletResponse.setHeader(...) before writing the rendered output.

      Parameters:
      name - The header name. Must not be null or blank.
      value - The header value. Must not be null.
      Returns:
      A new FreemarkerView carrying the additional response header.
      Throws:
      IllegalArgumentException - If name is null or blank, or value is null.

    • getTemplateName

      public String getTemplateName()
      Description copied from interface: View
      The name of the template to render — typically a path relative to the mixin-configured base path.

      For example, "hello.jsp" with a JSP mixin basePath("/WEB-INF/views/") resolves to "/WEB-INF/views/hello.jsp" on the request dispatcher.

      Specified by:
      getTemplateName in interface View
      Returns:
      The template name. Never null.

    • getAttributes

      public Map<String,Object> getAttributes()
      Description copied from interface: View
      The attributes to expose to the template.

      Each entry is forwarded to the templating engine as a named binding accessible from inside the template (e.g. ${name} in JSP / EL, th:text="${name}" in Thymeleaf).

      Specified by:
      getAttributes in interface View
      Returns:
      The attributes map. Never null; returns an immutable empty map when no attributes are bound.

    • getResponseHeaders

      public Map<String,String> getResponseHeaders()
      Description copied from interface: View
      Optional response headers applied by the renderer before forwarding to the templating engine.

      Typical use cases: setting Content-Type (e.g. "text/html; charset=UTF-8") or Cache-Control on the rendered response. The default implementation returns an empty map so most View impls don't need to override it.

      Specified by:
      getResponseHeaders in interface View
      Returns:
      An immutable map of headers to apply. Never null; defaults to an empty map.

    • toString

      public String toString()
      Overrides:
      toString in class Object
